abstract.t: This document specifies the Terminal Window Identity Protocol (TWIP), a unified identity model and discovery protocol for terminal panes, tabs, and windows across terminal emulators, shells, and multiplexers. TWIP defines a canonical identifier ("twid"), a normalized schema for legacy identifiers (for example, tty, process and session IDs, emulator-specific IDs, and multiplexer IDs), and standardized discovery surfaces via environment variables, OSC query and reply, and optional local IPC APIs. TWIP also defines lifecycle events, capability advertisement, security and privacy controls, and conformance levels.

section.name: Introduction
section.t: Terminal automation and observability workflows currently rely on fragmented identity signals: kernel PTY and TTY paths, process and session metadata, terminal-emulator-specific environment variables, multiplexer-specific identifiers, and optional GUI window metadata.
PTY and session terminology in this document follows common POSIX process and terminal conventions .
These signals are not standardized into one interoperable object. TWIP defines a common protocol for deriving, representing, and querying terminal runtime identity.

section.name: Conventions and Terminology
section.t: The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", "SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "NOT RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" in this document are to be interpreted as described in BCP 14 when, and only when, they appear in all capitals, as shown here.

dl.dd: A GUI tab within a terminal window.
A visual terminal region bound to one active PTY stream.
A runtime grouping defined by an emulator or multiplexer.
The canonical TWIP pane identity, encoded as UUIDv7 .
A non-negative integer incremented when identity is explicitly reconstituted (for example, clone or restore with rotation).

ol.li: Provide one canonical twid per pane lifetime.
Normalize legacy identity sources into one schema.
Support portable local automation and telemetry tooling.
Operate with or without GUI and multiplexers.
Emit lifecycle events for machine-consumable transitions.
Replacing kernel PTY or process semantics.
Mandating GUI metadata exposure by default.

section.name: Protocol Overview
section.t: TWIP defines three layers:

ol.li: Discovery Surfaces: environment variables, OSC, optional IPC.
Event and Capability Model: lifecycle streams and feature flags.

section.t: A conformant implementation MAY expose only a subset of discovery surfaces, subject to conformance levels in .

section.name: Canonical Identity Object
section.t: A TWIP identity object MUST include these top-level fields:

section.name: Scope Object
section.t: The scope object SHOULD include:

ol.li: twid MUST remain stable for a pane lifetime.
twid MUST rotate when a pane is duplicated into a distinct runtime object.
incarnation MUST increase when explicit identity reconstitution occurs.

section.name: Legacy Identifier Normalization
section.t: Each observed legacy identifier MUST be represented as a tuple with key, value, source, confidence, and observed_at.
Required fields:

ol.li: source (kernel, process, env, multiplexer, or gui)

section.t: Recommended key values:

section.name: Environment Variables
section.t: Implementations SHOULD expose:

section.name: OSC Query and Reply
section.t: Until control-sequence allocation is standardized, implementations MAY use a vendor-private OSC envelope.

ol.li: reply body: Base64URL-encoded UTF-8 JSON identity object

section.t: Receivers MUST ignore unknown fields.

section.name: Local IPC API
section.t: If TERMINAL_TWIP_SOCKET is present, it MUST reference a local authenticated socket serving JSON.
Required endpoints:

section.name: Lifecycle Event Model
section.t: Event emitters SHOULD publish:
Each event MUST include twid, timestamp, event_seq, and event_type.

section.name: Capability Advertisement
section.t: /v1/capabilities MUST include:

section.name: Remote Propagation
section.t: Cross-host propagation MUST be disabled by default.
When explicitly enabled, senders MAY forward:
Receivers MUST NOT overwrite local twid; they MAY append origin linkage metadata.

ol.li: Local IPC MUST enforce same-user filesystem permission checks.
Session-bound tokens SHOULD be used for IPC clients.
Implementations MUST treat OSC payloads as untrusted input.
Event subscribers SHOULD be isolated from mutation endpoints.
GUI title and handle exposure MUST be opt-in.
Raw hostnames SHOULD NOT be exposed by default; pseudonymous host_id SHOULD be used.
Forwarded origin fields SHOULD be minimized in multi-hop paths.
Implementations SHOULD support per-field redaction policies.

section.name: Level 1 (Core)
section.t: Required:

ol.li: identity object with twid, created_at, incarnation, scope, and legacy;

section.name: Level 2 (Observable)
section.t: Level 1 plus capability endpoint and lifecycle event stream.

section.name: Level 3 (Full)
section.t: Level 2 plus multiplexer normalization, GUI privacy controls, and explicit remote propagation controls.

section.name: IANA Considerations
section.t: This document has no IANA actions in this revision.
Future revisions may request standardized terminal control- sequence allocation for TWIP query and reply semantics.

ol.li: Default confidence calibration for GUI-derived fields.
